finding.contract_claim: record_token_usage reads result.usage.get("input_tokens") and result.usage.get("output_tokens"), implying CompletionResult exposes a `usage` mapping attribute.
finding.observed_behavior: The CompletionResult dataclass defines only content, input_tokens, output_tokens, model and stop_reason — there is no `usage` attribute, so lines 18-19 raise AttributeError the first time record_token_usage is called.

finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: `CompletionResult` is a dataclass with fields `content`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `model`, and `stop_reason`. There is no `usage` attribute. Lines 18-19 call `result.usage.get(...)`, which will raise `AttributeError: 'CompletionResult' object has no attribute 'usage'` on the first invocation of `record_token_usage`. Significance: This is a crash-on-call latent bug that makes the function completely non-functional despite appearing correct. Actionability: Replace `result.usage.get("input_tokens", 0)` with `result.input_tokens` and `result.usage.get("output_tokens", 0)` with `result.output_tokens`.
finding.suggested_fix: Replace `result.usage.get("input_tokens", 0)` with `result.input_tokens` and `result.usage.get("output_tokens", 0)` with `result.output_tokens` to match the actual CompletionResult dataclass fields.
